signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Jobs Data
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 spread at 1.536% marks a critical divergence as NZD outperforms (+0.613%) while CAD collapses (-0.923%) following a downside surprise in Canadian employment data. RBNZ 2.25% vs BoC 2.25% (0bp carry) leaves the pair purely driven by growth and labor differentials. CAD weakness is the primary G8 laggard driver, while NZD strength persists ahead of the RBNZ meeting. Implication: CAD underperformance persists until labor market stabilization; NZD/CAD upside remains the dominant G8 trend.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) shows speculators are heavily positioned against the kiwi despite an OIS hike bias for the May 27 meeting. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% (150bp USD carry) currently supports USD, but the crowded spec short creates significant squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise or formalizes the hike path. NZD/USD at 0.5963 is testing the upper bound of recent spec positioning. Implication: Short-unwind risk targets 0.6100 on hawkish RBNZ signaling.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is at an extreme 88% short. This 76-point divergence between institutional longs and retail faders typically precedes a retail capitulation event. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% (+60bp AUD carry) provides structural support for the institutional long thesis. Implication: Retail short-covering likely to accelerate the move toward 0.7350 if price holds above 0.7200.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Energy Shock
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.720 vs -0.125 norm (z=+1.78σ) indicates a breakdown in the traditional relationship as both assets respond to energy-driven inflation shocks. ECB's Lagarde confirms higher energy costs will push up input prices, keeping the 2% vs 3.75% (-175bp) rate deficit in focus. The positive correlation suggests EUR is trading as a 'proxy' for European energy security rather than a standard cyclical asset. Watch for the 1.1800 level to act as a pivot for this new regime.
